name: Marcus
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional. Distinct from Patrick exemplar (illness/contagion focus rather than injury/performance).

entries.situation: Felt a lump in his throat
entries.child_behavior_observed: Marcus was sure he had throat cancer and asked me to feel it and look down his throat with a flashlight.
entries.parent_response: I looked and told him it was normal, then we googled it together.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Marcus asked if it could be a brain tumor and wanted to go to the ER.
entries.parent_response: I gave him Tylenol and promised to take him to the doctor if it didn't go away.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Marcus refused to use the water fountain and washed his hands until they were raw, scared he'd catch it.
entries.parent_response: I let him bring his own water bottle and didn't push the hand washing.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Marcus said he might have appendicitis and shouldn't go to school.
entries.parent_response: I let him stay home and rest, and took his temperature a few times.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Marcus checked it in the mirror repeatedly and asked me daily whether it was getting bigger.
entries.parent_response: I checked it each day and reassured him it looked the same.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Marcus said his heart felt weird and he thought he was having a heart attack, and wanted to lie down and not move.
entries.parent_response: I sat with him and we tracked his pulse until it slowed.

situations: Noticing bodily sensations/symptoms (throat lump, headache, fast heartbeat, stomach ache)
parental_responses: Pulse-tracking and accommodating avoidance of contagion
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct.
